data.title: Senator Warren warned that Elon Musk's X Money plan could jeopardize consumers, financial stability, and national security.
data.description: Senator Warren, the top Democrat on the Senate Banking Committee, warned that the launch of Musk's X Money plan could pose risks to consumers, financial stability, and national security, citing his track record of operating X. (SolanaFloor)

data.title: Morgan Stanley's MSBT Fund Attracts Over $100 Million Amid Competitive Fees
data.description:
Morgan Stanley's newly launched MSBT fund has rapidly garnered more than $100 million in investments, positioning itself as a formidable player in the financial market. According to CoinDesk, the fund's appeal is largely attributed to its competitive fee structure, which is currently the lowest in the market. This strategic move has intensified competition among financial giants, prompting rivals such as Goldman Sachs to reassess their offerings.

The MSBT fund's success underscores Morgan Stanley's ability to attract investors by providing cost-effective solutions in a highly competitive environment. By offering lower fees, the fund has managed to capture the attention of investors seeking to maximize their returns. This development has not gone unnoticed by other major financial institutions, which are now under pressure to innovate and offer similar competitive pricing to retain their market share. As the financial landscape continues to evolve, the introduction of the MSBT fund marks a significant shift in how investment firms approach fee structures, potentially setting a new standard for the industry.

data.title: Token-Based Payments Offer Programmable Spending Limits and Reduced Fees
data.description:
Token-based payments present a transformative approach to financial transactions by allowing programmable spending limits and specifying which industries can utilize them. According to CoinDesk, this innovation has the potential to streamline financial processes by reducing the need for audits and lowering transaction fees through the elimination of intermediaries.

The ability to program spending limits into token-based payments offers a level of control and customization that traditional payment methods lack. This feature can be particularly beneficial for businesses and consumers looking to manage their expenditures more effectively. By setting predefined limits, users can ensure that their spending aligns with their financial goals and constraints, thereby promoting responsible financial behavior.

Moreover, the specification of industries that can use these token-based payments adds another layer of efficiency and security. By restricting usage to certain sectors, businesses can mitigate risks associated with unauthorized transactions and ensure compliance with industry-specific regulations. This targeted approach not only enhances security but also fosters trust among users and stakeholders.

The reduction of transaction fees is another significant advantage of token-based payments. By removing intermediaries from the payment process, businesses can save on costs typically associated with traditional financial transactions. This cost-effectiveness can lead to increased profitability and competitiveness in the market, as companies can allocate resources more efficiently.

Overall, token-based payments represent a promising development in the financial sector, offering enhanced control, security, and cost savings. As industries continue to explore and adopt this technology, the potential for widespread impact on financial operations and consumer behavior becomes increasingly apparent.

data.title: Benjamin Cowen on Crypto’s Next Phase: Why Liquidity, Market Cycles and Real Utility Matter More Than Hype
data.description:
In Episode 1 of Inside the Blockchain 100, “The Mathematics of Crypto,” Binance’s flagship series spotlighting voices shaping the future of Web3, macro analyst and Into The Cryptoverse founder Benjamin Cowen laid out a sober, data-driven view of the crypto market. Drawing on his background in mathematics, physics and nuclear engineering, Cowen argued that Bitcoin is still largely following historical cycle behavior — even if this cycle feels different from previous ones.
His central point: the four-year cycle is not dead. In fact, Cowen said Bitcoin “topped when it always does,” noting that major peaks have historically arrived in “Q4 of 2013, Q4 of 2017, Q4 of 2021, and now Q4 of 2025.” What changed, in his view, was not the timing, but the market psychology. Unlike prior cycle tops driven by retail euphoria, this one “topped on apathy rather than euphoria.”
That distinction matters because, according to Cowen, it helps explain why the market did not get the usual post-Bitcoin rotation into higher-risk altcoins. In previous cycles, strong retail participation helped drive an “alt season” after Bitcoin peaked. This time, social interest and retail momentum were weaker, so that rotation never meaningfully materialized. He compared the current environment to 2019, another period when Bitcoin topped on apathy and did not trigger broad speculative upside in altcoins.
Liquidity, Not Hype, Is Driving the Market
For Cowen, the bigger driver is macroeconomics, not crypto-native storytelling. He tied the current market structure to tight liquidity and a late business-cycle backdrop, arguing that under these conditions “risk rolled down the curve rather than up the curve.” In practical terms, that means capital moved toward relatively safer assets inside crypto, with Bitcoin holding up better than much of the altcoin market.
He also pointed to similarities with 2019 in relation to Federal Reserve policy. Bitcoin, he said, peaked about two months before quantitative tightening ended in both periods. The issue is not that liquidity is absent, but that it is not improving fast enough to quickly reverse the broader downturn. As a result, he sees Bitcoin in a slower, grinding bear phase rather than a sudden post-blowoff collapse.
Even so, Cowen suggested the current drawdown remains broadly consistent with historical mid-cycle patterns. He argued that Bitcoin is “tracking prior midterm years” fairly closely and reiterated that bear markets often involve deceptive rallies. In his words, “in bear markets we spend more time trending up than trending down,” which can trap both overly bullish and overly bearish investors.
Zoom Out: Markets Are Bigger Than Daily Noise
On market method, Cowen strongly pushed back against short-term prediction culture. “Short-term price action is akin to a random walk,” he said, adding that “you cannot hope to predict it.” Instead, he prefers to focus on larger cycles and momentum, which he sees as one of the few forms of technical analysis with real value. His advice was to “zoom out,” rely less on emotional narratives and pay closer attention to longer-term structure.
That skepticism also extends to crypto narratives. Cowen argued repeatedly that “narratives follow price,” not the other way around. ETF launches, macro headlines, oil shocks and institutional adoption stories may dominate discussion in real time, but he believes markets typically price such themes in well before they become popular explanations. In hindsight, he said, people can always invent reasons for a move that was already underway.
Crypto Needs Utility, Not Just Speculation
One of the sharpest parts of the interview came when Cowen discussed the state of crypto itself. He warned that too much capital in this cycle flowed toward speculative sectors, especially meme coins, rather than products with durable utility. “The future of crypto is not, it should not be meme coins,” he said. More broadly, he argued the industry became too focused on “how do we get more money into the market” instead of “how do we make crypto better.”
That critique led to his takeaway: crypto needs real use cases to reach mass adoption. For Cowen, mainstream users do not yet rely on crypto in the way they rely on the internet, smartphones or increasingly AI tools. To change that, the industry must build products people genuinely need, not “create the product and then make up a need for it.”
AI and Stablecoins Could Help Define the Next Phase
Among the sectors he believes could help bridge that gap, AI stood out most clearly. Cowen said crypto could become relevant in an AI-driven economy where autonomous agents transact, pay humans for tasks and use blockchain rails for fast settlement. He also highlighted stablecoins as an already credible example of blockchain utility.
In closing, Cowen struck a cautiously optimistic tone. While he expects many speculative narratives to fail and many altcoins to fade over time, he believes the cleansing effect of a bear market can leave the asset class healthier. His long-term principle for investors was simple: “bears sound smart, but bulls make money.”

data.title: JustLend DAO Executes Third JST Buyback and Burn
data.description: JustLend DAO has successfully completed its third JST buyback and burn, eliminating 271,337,579 JST valued at $21.3 million. According to NS3.AI, this action brings the total amount of JST burned to over 1.356 billion, representing 13.70% of the total supply. JustLend DAO stated that the buyback and burn were funded by quarterly net profits and carried-over earnings. The organization plans to maintain this initiative on a quarterly basis.

data.title: Market news: Anthropic plans to grant UK banks access to "Mythos" next week.
data.description: Market news: Anthropic plans to grant UK banks access to "Mythos" next week. (Jinshi)

data.title: Turkey to Host Regional Security Talks with Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, and Egypt
data.description: Turkey is set to host discussions later this week aimed at establishing a regional security platform with Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, and potentially Egypt. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the significance of these talks in fostering cooperation among these nations. The initiative seeks to address regional security challenges and enhance collaborative efforts in maintaining stability. The inclusion of Egypt in the discussions remains uncertain, but the talks underscore Turkey's role in regional diplomacy. The meeting is expected to cover various security issues pertinent to the region, with an emphasis on strengthening ties and mutual understanding among the participating countries.

data.title: Dubai Enhances Flexibility for Hedge Funds Amid Iran Conflict Disruptions
data.description: Dubai is taking swift action to provide hedge funds with increased flexibility in response to disruptions caused by the ongoing conflict involving Iran, according to sources. Bloomberg posted on X that the measures aim to mitigate the impact of the geopolitical tensions on financial operations. The move is part of a broader strategy to maintain stability in the region's financial markets and support investment activities. As the situation evolves, Dubai continues to adapt its regulatory framework to ensure that hedge funds can effectively manage risks and capitalize on opportunities. This development underscores Dubai's commitment to reinforcing its position as a leading financial hub in the Middle East.

data.title: Bahrain Faces Challenges Amid Iran Conflict
data.description:
Bahrain has been grappling with significant challenges even before the recent conflict involving Iran erupted. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the economic and political pressures that have been mounting in the region. The ongoing tensions have exacerbated existing issues, impacting Bahrain's stability and economic prospects.

The geopolitical landscape in the Middle East has been volatile, with Bahrain finding itself in a precarious position due to its proximity to Iran and the broader regional dynamics. The conflict has intensified concerns over security and economic stability, further complicating Bahrain's efforts to navigate these turbulent times.

As the situation unfolds, Bahrain's government is working to address the immediate impacts of the conflict while also focusing on long-term strategies to bolster its economy and maintain stability. The international community is closely monitoring the developments, given the potential implications for regional and global security.

data.title: STOCKS | Banks Channel Excess Capital into Wall Street Expansion
data.description: Banks are increasingly directing their surplus capital towards expanding their operations on Wall Street. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X that this trend reflects a strategic shift as financial institutions seek to bolster their presence in the lucrative financial hub. The move comes amid a backdrop of evolving market dynamics and regulatory landscapes, prompting banks to reassess their capital allocation strategies. By investing in Wall Street businesses, banks aim to enhance their competitive edge and capitalize on emerging opportunities in the financial sector. This development underscores the ongoing transformation within the banking industry as institutions adapt to changing economic conditions and strive for growth.

data.title: Brent Crude's Weak Rebound Amid Falling Open Interest
data.description: Brent crude oil prices hovered around $94.92, with open interest experiencing a significant drop of approximately 30%, decreasing from over 700,000 to 491,810. According to NS3.AI, the recent 5% price increase from approximately $90.29 is considered a weak rebound. This assessment is based on the observation that trading volume has declined, and options activity appears to be more focused on conflict hedging rather than directional buying.

data.title: South Korea to Pilot Tokenized Deposits for Government Spending
data.description: South Korea's Ministry of Economy and Finance has announced a pilot program to utilize tokenized deposits for government operational spending, aiming for a full rollout by the fourth quarter of 2026. According to NS3.AI, the trial will commence in Sejong City, focusing on testing preset limits related to timing and spending categories within a distributed ledger technology (DLT) regulatory sandbox. This sandbox approval enables the ministry to experiment with tokenized deposits for fund execution, despite current regulations mandating the use of government cards.

data.title: Bitcoin Trades Near $75,000 Amid Fed Leadership Transition Concerns
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has stated his intention to dismiss Jerome Powell if Powell does not step down when his term as Federal Reserve Chair concludes on May 15. According to NS3.AI, Bitcoin is currently trading near $75,000, reflecting market uncertainty surrounding the potential change in Fed leadership. Kraken's chief economist, Thomas Perfumo, has expressed concerns that markets are underestimating the impact of this transition, suggesting it could significantly influence cryptoassets. Perfumo highlighted that Kevin Warsh's confirmation hearing on April 21 might serve as a pivotal moment for traders to reevaluate their expectations regarding interest rate cuts.

data.title: Future Competition to Focus on Fixed Rates, Compliance Collateral, and Institutional Credit Assessment
data.description: The future landscape of competition is expected to center around three key areas: fixed interest rates, compliance collateral, and institutional credit assessment. According to PANews, these sectors are anticipated to drive significant developments and strategic focus in the coming years. As financial markets evolve, stakeholders are likely to prioritize these elements to enhance stability and growth. The emphasis on fixed rates aims to provide predictability and security in financial transactions, while compliance collateral ensures adherence to regulatory standards. Institutional credit assessment is set to play a crucial role in evaluating creditworthiness and managing risk effectively.

data.title: South Korea pilots blockchain deposit tokens for government spending.
data.description: South Korea is piloting a blockchain-based deposit token for government spending. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Guotou Silver LOF Announces Temporary Trading Suspension
data.description: Guotou Silver LOF has announced a temporary suspension of trading starting from April 17, 2026, until 10:30 on the same day. According to Jin10, the suspension will not affect redemption services, which will continue as usual during this period. Trading will resume at 10:30 on April 17, 2026.

data.title: Canadian Home Prices Continue Decline Amid Global Uncertainty
data.description:
Canadian home prices have fallen for the 16th consecutive month, driven by rising mortgage rates due to global uncertainty from the Middle East conflict. According to Jin10, data released by the Canadian Real Estate Association on Thursday showed that the benchmark price in March decreased by 0.4% from February, reaching 659,100 CAD. This marks the lowest level since March 2021 and continues the downward trend that began at the end of 2024. 

Senior economist Shaun Cathcart from the association stated, "The mid-month surge in fixed mortgage rates related to rising inflation has exacerbated the already unstable economic conditions at the start of the year. The timing of the mortgage rate increase, coupled with the perception that it might be temporary, could deter potential buyers during the most active months of April, May, and June, as they wait for rates to decline." 

The real estate board's data also indicated that home sales in Canada slightly decreased by 0.1% in March compared to the previous month, while the number of newly listed homes dropped by 0.2%.

data.title: Yuan's Global Ambitions Hindered by Capital Controls and Convertibility Issues
data.description: The Chinese yuan's potential to become a global currency faces significant challenges due to existing capital controls, offshore limitations, and convertibility gaps. According to CoinDesk, these barriers continue to impede the yuan's internationalization efforts, despite China's ambitions to elevate its currency on the world stage. The yuan's journey towards global prominence is complicated by stringent capital controls that restrict the free flow of money in and out of China. These controls are designed to stabilize the domestic economy but simultaneously limit the yuan's appeal as a global currency. Offshore trading of the yuan is also constrained, further hindering its international use. The currency's convertibility issues add another layer of complexity, as the yuan is not fully convertible on the capital account, making it less attractive for international investors and businesses. China's efforts to promote the yuan globally include initiatives such as establishing offshore yuan trading centers and encouraging its use in international trade. However, these measures have yet to overcome the fundamental obstacles posed by capital controls and convertibility restrictions. The yuan's path to becoming a major global currency remains fraught with challenges, requiring significant policy adjustments and reforms to achieve its internationalization goals.

data.title: Binance to Host Quiz Challenge with $200 Prize Pool
data.description:
Binance announced on X that it will host an interactive quiz challenge as part of its educational series, offering participants a chance to win a share of a $200 prize pool. The event, titled 'Quiz Challenge EP14,' invites all users to engage in a quick question-and-answer session, with rewards awaiting the top 20 participants.

The live session is scheduled to take place on April 16 at 20:00 (HKT), featuring host Soda. Participants can look forward to an engaging experience, with the opportunity to earn rewards based on their knowledge and interaction during the event. Additionally, the live stream will feature a special 'Red Packet' giveaway, where participants can trigger a prize ranging from $100 to $200 by reaching certain engagement and interaction milestones during the broadcast.

This initiative is part of Binance's ongoing efforts to enhance user engagement through educational and interactive content. By participating in the quiz challenge, users can not only test their knowledge but also potentially earn rewards, making it an attractive opportunity for both new and experienced users. The event underscores Binance's commitment to fostering a dynamic and interactive community, encouraging users to actively participate and benefit from their involvement in the platform's activities.

data.title: U.S. Cryptocurrency Market Structure Act Nears Final Negotiation Stage
data.description:
On April 16, analysts from JPMorgan indicated that negotiations over the U.S. Cryptocurrency Market Structure Act, known as the CLARITY Act, are approaching their final phase. According to BlockBeats, the discussions have narrowed down to a few remaining contentious issues, with stablecoin reward topics reportedly progressing well. Despite banks expressing concerns about stablecoins offering deposit-like returns, there is a bipartisan compromise emerging. JPMorgan acknowledges that while no legislation is perfect, the act's passage would provide crucial regulatory clarity for integrating digital assets into the U.S. financial system.

Currently, the Cryptocurrency Market Structure Act is in advanced negotiation stages within the U.S. Senate. Senate staff have noted that the draft is "very close" to resolution, although the final text has not been released, nor has a formal vote been scheduled. The remaining major disagreements focus on stablecoin rewards, DeFi regulation, and token classification. Although optimism is rising, the act faces potential delays due to the 2026 midterm elections, which could introduce a more uncertain political environment. If passed, the act would delineate regulatory authority between the SEC and CFTC, establishing a long-term regulatory framework for stablecoins, DeFi, and the broader crypto industry.
